title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Life Insurance for Obese or Overweight People
Obesity or being overweight is a common lifestyle condition present in a major percentage of the population. According to the data¹ published by the National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5), the prevalence of obesity has increased in 2019-21 compared to 2015-16. The data states the following facts –
One in every four Indians is overweight. The figure was one in five during 2015-16
The percentage of obese men between 15 and 49 years of age increased to 23% from the previous 19%. In the case of women, the figure stood at 24% from the previous 21%.
Even WHO reports stated that the incidence of obesity grew three times between 1975 and 2016.
Obesity brings with it a range of health complications which might also result in death. According to the statistics presented by the Global Burden of Diseases¹, obesity was the reason for premature death for about 5.02 million people worldwide in 2019. Given the risks associated with obesity, do life insurance plans offer coverage to obese or overweight people?
Premium Rates of Life Insurance for Overweight People
Yes, life insurance companies allow coverage for obese or overweight people. However, the coverage comes with an additional premium. Given the increased health and mortality risk with obesity, life insurance companies charge additional life insurance premiums for the coverage. The premium rates depend on the insurer and its underwriting policies.
In some cases, the coverage might also be restricted if the insured has an abnormally high weight. Insurers might also insist on a pre-entrance medical check-up to assess the health before granting coverage to overweight or obese people.
Associated Risks with Life Insurance for Obese People
If an individual is obese or overweight, he might face the following risks –
Health issues
Increased risk of premature death
Due to such risks, availing of a comprehensive life insurance policy might be challenging. An obese individual might face the following risks under the policy –
Restricted coverage
Restricted tenure
Restriction on the scope of coverage, i.e., weight-related medical conditions might be excluded and claims might not be paid in the case of death due to such conditions
Limited availability of plans.
What is BMI or Body Mass Index?
The full form of BMI is Body Mass Index. The BMI measures your weight in proportion to your height. The calculated figure helps in understanding whether you are underweight, overweight or have a normal weight.
How to Calculate BMI²?
You can calculate your BMI using the BMI formula which is as follows -
BMI = Weight (in kilograms) / Height2 (in metres)
So, if your weight is 70 kilograms and height is 1.60 metres, your BMI would be 70 divided by the square of (1.60)2
This would come to
= 27.34
You can also use BMI calculators for a quick calculation.
There is a BMI table which shows the current position of your weight. Using the calculated BMI value, you can check the bracket in which you fall. Have a look
In the above example, as the BMI is 27.34, you would be considered overweight.
How does BMI Affect your Life Insurance?
Underwriters assess your BMI when underwriting your life insurance proposal. The BMI helps them check your weight range. If you fall under the normal weight range, the policy is issued without any restrictions and life insurance premium loadings. However, if your BMI falls in the overweight or obese category, the underwriter would do one or more of the following –
Add a loading on the premium,
Restrict the sum assured,
Impose a coverage restriction or
Reject the proposal.
Rejection usually happens in severe cases when the BMI is extremely high and the insured is highly obese. In other cases, the policy is issued after incorporating one or more of the aforementioned changes.
Conclusion
Being overweight or obese does not mean that you cannot avail yourself of a life insurance policy. The coverage is allowed but the premium is usually high and the coverage might also be restricted, both in terms of level and scope. So, use the BMI calculator and assess where your weight stands before buying the policy. Also, try and maintain a healthy weight to enjoy a comprehensive insurance policy.
Moreover, even if you are overweight or obese, do not lie about it or hide it when filling up the proposal form. Remember, life insurance policies are contracts of good faith. If you provide incorrect information in the proposal form, it might cause problems at the time of claims. In the case of a claim, if the insurer finds out that you hid or lied about your actual weight, it might reject the claim and dispute the coverage altogether. So, to avoid such situations, always be honest about your weight and other details when buying the insurance policy.
Check out the policies that you can avail of and their terms and conditions. Choose the right policy and secure your finances.
